pages.full_text:
the Common Market or the Dark Ages?
We had a coffee at a café. We had lunch in a field - friendly farmers this time, carrying in their large hay roles - "rolos" as John calls them.

    In the afternoon we came to
Cassaniouge    * * * * *. We have a lovely view looking over the lake, with an artificial swimming pool built on at the far side. The boys have enjoyed the swimming. There is an island in the middle of the lake. French boys took TW on a ride on their hired pedalo.
    We went out to supper in Calvinet, at Hotel de Beauségou. One of the most delicious meals - first course terrine de saumon et Saumon fumé. Recommended in "Off the Beaten Track".
    Enormous flying beetles & horseflies.
pages.notes: Page continues from previous spread mid-sentence ('the Common Market or the Dark Ages?'). Campsite name 'Cassaniouge' could also be read as 'Cassaniouze' — a village/campsite in the Cantal region of France on the Truyère river. 'Hotel de Beauségou' could also be 'Beauségour' or 'Beausejour' — handwriting is slightly ambiguous on the final letter. 'TW' appears to be initials of a child/family member.

Right page contains a detailed pencil architectural sketch of a chateau/castle building (Chateau de la Mèle), with no handwritten page number clearly visible — a faint '41' appears in the top-right corner but this seems inconsistent with the left page number (104); it may be a partial or different notation. The sketch shows a multi-storey building with towers, windows and surrounding structures. The label 'Chateau de la Mèle' is written in pencil below/beside the sketch. 'Mèle' could also read 'Mélo' or 'Mele' — the accent is faint.
